selection_reasons.cross-examine-falsify-5x3: current panel winner retained unchanged for replication
selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 27 fresh panels: 146/354 exact; 36.4% weakest family

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 30 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged as the principal replicated answer-generating repair control.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Reintroduce the previously tested sequential cross-examination architecture as the orthogonal lane. Against the same five-answer bank and three-call depth as the champion, it tests whether passing a falsifiable proposal and critique improves integration more than independent parallel repair.
strategies.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

strategies.hypothesis: Continue the matched efficiency probe: one answer-generating falsifier costs two fewer calls per case and retained a 4–0 helpful-to-harmful balance on the checkpoint.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Continue the cheaper full-bank candidate-only selector to measure whether a single falsifier can reliably convert the nine-bank coverage advantage; its large current oracle gap makes this a direct selector stress test.
strategies.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection
